q: Biology is broadly divided into five major themes: organization, information, energy and matter, interactions and evolution.
Biology is broadly divided into four major themes: organization, information, energy, and evolution.
The hierarchy of life on earth is unfolded as follows: biosphere, ecosystem, community, population, organism, organ system, organ, tissue, cell, organelle, molecule and atom.
The hierarchy of life on earth begins with the organism and ends with the biosphere.
With each set up in the biological hierarchy, new properties emerge because of interactions among components at the lower levels.
New properties emerge at successive levels of biological organization due to the isolation of components at lower levels.
The structure and function of biological components are interrelated.
The structure and function of biological components are independent of each other.
The cell is the lowest level of organization that can perform all activities required for life.
The tissue is the lowest level of organization that can perform all activities required for life.
Cells are either prokaryote or eukaryote.
Cells are either prokaryote or virus.
Eukaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles, including DNA in nucleus.
Prokaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles, including DNA in nucleus.
Prokaryotic cells lack membrane-enclosed organelles.
Prokaryotic cells possess membrane-enclosed organelles but lack a nucleus.
The genetic information is encoded in the nucleotide sequences of DNA.
The genetic information is encoded in the amino acid sequences of proteins.
The DNA transmits heritable information from parents to offspring.
The RNA transmits heritable information from parents to offspring without the involvement of DNA.
DNA sequences called genes act as instructions to make a cell's protein production by being transcribed into mRNA and then translated into specific proteins.
DNA sequences called genes act as instructions to make a cell's protein production by being translated directly into specific proteins without mRNA transcription.
Gene expression can also produce some RNAs that are not translated into proteins but serve other important function.
Gene expression always produces RNAs that are translated into proteins and serve no other function.
Energy flows through the ecosystem.
Energy cycles through the ecosystem in a closed loop without any loss.
All organisms require energy for their life.
Only producers require energy for their life in an ecosystem.
Producers convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy.
Consumers convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy in an ecosystem.
Some of the energy passed to consumers is lost from the ecosystem as heat.
All energy passed to consumers is retained within the ecosystem without any heat loss.
Chemical cycle occurs between organisms and the environment.
Chemical cycle occurs only within organisms and never involves the environment.
Organisms interact continuously with physical factors.
Organisms interact only with other living organisms and not with physical factors.
Plants take up nutrients from the soil and chemicals from the air and use energy from the sun.
Plants take up all required nutrients exclusively from the air and do not use energy from the sun.
In feedback regulation, a process is regulated by its output or product.
In feedback regulation, a process is regulated exclusively by external factors unrelated to its output or product.
In negative feedback, accumulation of the product slows its production.
In negative feedback, accumulation of the product speeds up its production.
In positive feedback, a product speeds up its own production.
In positive feedback, a product slows down its own production.
Feedback is a type of regulation common to life at all levels, from molecules to ecosystems.
Feedback is a type of regulation found only at the organism level and not at molecular or ecosystem levels.
Evolution is the process of change that has transformed life on Earth, accounts for the unity and diversity of life.
Evolution is a static process that maintains life on Earth in an unchanging state.
Evolution explains evolutionary adaptation and the match of organisms to their environment.
Evolution explains why organisms are completely mismatched to their environments.
Biologists classify species according to a system of broader and broader groups.
Biologists classify species according to a system of narrower and narrower groups only.
Domain Bacteria and Archaea consist of prokaryotes.
Domain Bacteria and Eukarya consist of prokaryotes.
Domain Eukarya includes various groups of protists as well as fungi, plants, and animals.
Domain Eukarya includes only animals and plants, excluding protists and fungi.
Darwin (1809-1882) proposed natural selection as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of populations to their environments.
Darwin (1809-1882) proposed genetic drift as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of populations to their environments.

Natural selection is defined as the evolutionary process that occurs when a population is exposed to environmental factors that consistently cause individuals with certain heritable traits to have greater reproductive success than do individuals with other heritable traits.
Natural selection is defined as the evolutionary process that occurs when individuals with certain heritable traits always have lower reproductive success regardless of environmental factors.
